message: I've been thinking about how heat waves can sneak up on us, and honestly, it's one of those things that's easy to overlook until you're in the middle of one. I found a really solid resource from Ready.gov that breaks down the practical stuff—like how to stay comfortable at home, what to watch for with neighbors and family, and just general sense-making for hot weather. Nothing scary, just useful information I wish I'd had bookmarked years ago. What's your go-to move when the temperature really climbs—are you more of a "stay inside" person or do you have other strategies that work for you?
